latin: Hobbiti ad cauponam nomine 'Equus Subsultans' in oppido Bree pervenerunt. ibi homo quidam obscurus in angulo sedebat et eos observabat. nomen eius erat Vagus, sed verum nomen Aragorn erat. hic vir Hobbittos de periculo monuit, nam servi Sauronis eos iam quaerebant. Aragorn se ducem obtulit ut eos per terras silvestres tuto duceret. quamquam aspectu sordidus videbatur, Frodo ei credere constituit.

questions.prompt: What was the name of the inn?
Why did Aragorn warn the Hobbits?
questions.target: Sauron's servants were already searching for them
choices: Sauron's servants were already searching for them

questions.prompt: What did Aragorn offer to do?
questions.target: Guide them safely through the wild lands
choices: Guide them safely through the wild lands
Send them back to the Shire

translation: The Hobbits arrived at an inn called 'The Prancing Pony' in the town of Bree. There a certain dark man sat in a corner and was watching them. His name was Strider, but his true name was Aragorn. This man warned the Hobbits about danger, for Sauron's servants were already seeking them. Aragorn offered himself as a guide to lead them safely through the wild lands. Although he appeared rough in appearance, Frodo decided to trust him.
